summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that the ExecuteCommand node executes arbitrary shell commands on the host system without sanitization or validation. The PoC creates a workflow with a malicious command payload that successfully creates a proof file demonstrating arbitrary command execution.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction pipeline should create a workflow with an ExecuteCommand node containing a malicious command, execute it, and produce a proof file (/tmp/pwned.txt) containing the verification marker, demonstrating arbitrary command execution on the n8n host.
actual_observed_result: The reproduction successfully built the n8n environment, created a workflow with the ExecuteCommand node, executed the malicious command payload 'id > /tmp/pwned.txt && echo EVOHUNT-CMD-INJECTION-VERIFIED > /tmp/pwned.txt', and created the proof file /tmp/pwned.txt with the verification marker, confirming arbitrary command execution.
verification_method: The run script checks for the presence of /tmp/pwned.txt created by the malicious command and verifies its contents contain the expected verification marker. This distinguishes actual command execution from workflow execution failures or unrelated crashes.
evidence_produced_by_run: File /tmp/pwned.txt was created by the malicious command payload
File contents contain the verification marker 'EVOHUNT-CMD-INJECTION-VERIFIED'
The workflow execution succeeded and the ExecuteCommand node processed the malicious command
No sanitization or validation prevented the command injection

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh copies the target repository from the session directory and mounts that copy into the container at /target-repo.
